enriched_points.point_title: Use in patients with EGFR mutations
enriched_points.expanded_content: Erlotinib targets EGFR mutations, demonstrating substantial efficacy in non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) patients, significantly improving treatment outcomes for those with specific genetic markers.
Clinical trials, including phase II studies, show erlotinib enhances progression-free survival compared to standard therapies, establishing its role as a first-line treatment for advanced NSCLC.

Erlotinib offers advantages such as reduced side effects, improved quality of life, and longer survival rates compared to traditional chemotherapy regimens, particularly in EGFR mutation-positive patients.
